target.title: Defer merged menu close rebuilds
target.body:
Refs #1274.

This targets the remaining close/dismiss freeze reported after #1277. cc @giuseppebisemi because this PR specifically addresses the close-path stack you profiled in #1274.

## Summary
- Reproduces the close-path regression with a focused menu lifecycle test: stale merged menus were being rebuilt after `menuDidClose`, including through deferred interaction refresh follow-up.
- Marks stale merged menus as deferred-until-next-open when they close, so `populateMenu` / SwiftUI hosting teardown and rebuild do not run on the user dismiss path.
- Keeps existing closed-menu preparation for other attached menus and for invalidations that did not originate from closing the merged menu.

## Behavior proof
Local macOS/AppKit lifecycle proof, rerun after the latest push on 2026-06-03 14:13 +0800:

```text
swift test --filter "CodexBarTests.StatusMenuTests/`merged menu close defers stale rebuild until next open`()"
✔ Test "merged menu close defers stale rebuild until next open" passed after 0.333 seconds.
✔ Test run with 1 test in 1 suite passed after 0.334 seconds.
```

The regression test constructs the merged `NSMenu`, marks it fresh, opens it, invalidates it while open, then closes it. The post-close assertions verify no closed-menu rebuild task is scheduled and the menu version remains at the opened version; the subsequent `menuWillOpen` assertion verifies the stale content refresh happens on reopen instead of on dismiss.

This is not a full live desktop recording. It is behavior proof for the AppKit menu lifecycle path that was rebuilding after close. The broader `populateMenu` / SwiftUI hosting cost still needs separate profiling and should remain tracked in #1274 until a shipped build or live recording confirms user-visible lag is gone.

## Notes
This does not claim to make `populateMenu` itself cheap. It removes the close/dismiss-path rebuild that can block the main thread; the deeper `MenuDescriptor.build` / SwiftUI hosting cost is still a separate optimization target.

## Validation
- `swift test --filter "CodexBarTests.StatusMenuTests/`merged menu close defers stale rebuild until next open`()"`
- `swift test --filter StatusMenuOpenRefreshTests`
- `make check`
